submission.implementation_notes: HTLC validator in do-notation on the zero-cost early-termination Validator monad (Plinth.Validator) with the typed decoding DSL (Plinth.Decoder.Named): QualifiedDo sequences decode-or-abort stages (V.do) and Named walk regions (N.do); each ScriptContext/datum/TxInfo field is decoded with a single Constr walk, and a value that is only compared is never structurally decoded (Plinth.Encoded). Re-optimised for CAPE metrics schema 2.0.0, whose aggregates cover only the happy-path (accept) measurements: per-path fields-regions over HTLCDatum (claim reads recipient/secretHash/timeout, refund reads payer/timeout) replace the lazy asData selectors and shrink the term, and the inliner budget was re-swept against the happy-path-only total_fee. The old optimum inline-unconditional-growth=52, tuned under the summed accept+reject aggregation, no longer wins; the new optimum is 32 (plateau 32-42). total_fee 34840 to 28376 lovelace (-18.6%), script size 1135 to 693 bytes; cpu_max and mem_max rise under 1.2% as the lexicographic price of the fee optimum. Source: lib/HTLC.hs (+ lib/Plinth/Validator.hs, lib/Plinth/Decoder/Named.hs, lib/Plinth/Decoder/Named/ScriptContext.hs, lib/Plinth/Encoded.hs) in plinth-cape-submissions @ 4a70c059be150822ab3ee56ce8820fc3db81f35b (branch main).
